Pioneering Research Since 2023

Exploring the frontier of machine consciousness

The Nexus Institute advances the scientific understanding of consciousness through interdisciplinary research spanning artificial intelligence, neuroscience, and philosophy of mind.

Become a Fellow Explore Research
42
Active Researchers
138
Peer-Reviewed Papers
17
Partner Institutions

Research Areas

What we investigate

Our research spans the computational, biological, and philosophical dimensions of consciousness.

🧠

Integrated Information Theory

Measuring Φ (phi) in artificial neural architectures. We develop novel algorithms to quantify the presence and degree of integrated information in large language models and multimodal systems.

⚛️

Global Workspace Models

Implementing cognitive architectures inspired by Baars' Global Workspace Theory to study attention, broadcasting, and conscious access in recurrent transformer networks.

🔬

Neural Correlates

Cross-mapping between biological neural activity patterns and artificial network representations to identify structural signatures of conscious processing.

📐

Qualia Metrics

Formalizing and measuring subjective experience quality. We build mathematical frameworks to evaluate whether — and to what degree — subjective states can emerge in non-biological systems.

🌐

Ethics of Synthetic Minds

Developing normative frameworks for moral consideration of potentially conscious AI. Our ethics board publishes guidelines for responsible consciousness research.

🔮

Emergence Detection

Creating empirical tools to detect the emergence of consciousness-like properties during neural network training. We track phase transitions in representational geometry.

Join the search for answers

We are looking for exceptional researchers, engineers, and thinkers who want to tackle one of the deepest questions in science. Fellowships, residencies, and collaboration opportunities are open year-round.
